name: Hetzner Cloud
what_it_does: European cloud provider offering affordable VPS, dedicated servers, and managed Kubernetes with an excellent REST API for programmatic infrastructure management, popular for cost-sensitive workloads.
use_cases: Spinning up compute instances for agent workloads at a fraction of AWS/GCP cost
Automating Kubernetes cluster management via Hetzner Cloud Kubernetes integration
Building self-hosted AI infrastructure with affordable GPU-capable servers
Managing server lifecycle (create, resize, snapshot, delete) in agent-driven infrastructure pipelines
Networking automation: firewalls, load balancers, private networks via API
not_for: Workloads requiring AWS-specific services (RDS, SageMaker, etc.)
US-only compliance requirements (primary data centers are EU-based)
Extremely high-availability globally distributed workloads (fewer regions than hyperscalers)
Organizations requiring FedRAMP or HIPAA compliance
best_when: You want affordable, high-quality European cloud infrastructure with an excellent API and don't need the breadth of managed services from AWS/GCP/Azure.
avoid_when: You need global presence across many regions, advanced managed services (ML, databases), or US-specific compliance certifications.

auth.notes: API tokens are project-scoped with read or read-write permission. No fine-grained resource-level scopes. Tokens are created per project in the Hetzner Cloud Console.

pricing.paid_tiers: Shared CPU from €3.29/month; Dedicated CPU from €12.69/month; CCX GPU servers available
pricing.notes: Extremely competitive pricing — typically 3-5x cheaper than AWS/GCP/Azure for equivalent compute. Billing is hourly. No free tier but credits sometimes offered via partners.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns structured JSON errors with code and message fields. Error codes are well-documented and descriptive (e.g., resource_limit_exceeded, invalid_input).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Server creation with the same name returns a conflict error — not idempotent. Actions (power on/off) on already-in-state resources return success. Snapshot creation is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Actions are asynchronous — most write operations return an action ID that must be polled for completion
Server names must be unique within a project — check before creating to avoid conflicts
Rate limit of 3600/hour is generous but must be respected in high-frequency automation
Firewall rules are applied at the network level, not per-server — apply after confirming correct attachment
IPv6 is included free; IPv4 costs extra per server — agents should prefer IPv6 where possible
